DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-11-2019 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Derek O. Myers, Esq. of Chauvel & Glatt, T:650.573.9500, for defendant/moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The tentative ruling on DEFENDANT ROBERT SCHMIDT, ROBERT'S TAX SERVICE, LLC's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT being uncontested was adopted by the Court, as follows:

The demurrer of Defendants Robert R. Schmidt and Robert's Tax Service, LLC to Plaintiff NextArts's first amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend on the ground that it is barred in its entirety by the statute of limitations.

Order signed in open court. (See word minutes and order for the Court's full ruling.)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-20-2019 Continued To Nov-20-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-09-2019 Continued To Oct-09-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief Regarding This Courts Order Sustaining Defendants Demurrer To Complaint Without Leave To Amend
Off Calendar Aug-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF NEXTARTS, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S Motion For Relief Regarding This Courts Order Sustaining Defendants Demurrer To Complaint Without Leave To Amend.

APPEARANCES: Atty. MICHAEL LIEBERMAN, ESQ. of Law Office of Michael Lieberman (Tel: 415-929-3197) appearing for the plaintiff;
Atty. DEREK O. MYERS, ESQ. of Chauvel & Glatt (Tel: 650-573-9500) appearing for defendants.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff NextArts's motion for relief regarding this Court's order sustaining defendants' demurrer to complaint without leave to amend is granted on the conditions that plaintiff's counsel (1) file the first amended complaint within 5 days of the Court's order and (2) pay defendants $1,500 to reimburse them for the costs they incurred in filing the demurrer and attending the hearing. Plaintiff's counsel failed to file a timely opposition to defendants' demurrer, believing (incorrectly) that he could do so at any time prior to the hearing on the demurrer. As he acknowledges, Code of Civil Procedure section 472 was amended effective January 1, 2016, to require that an amended complaint be filed and served "no later than the date for filing an opposition to the demurrer." Plaintiff's counsel then failed to file the proposed first amended complaint, instead merely attaching it to plaintiff's "response" to defendant's demurrer; and he then failed timely to appear at the hearing, although he had notified the Court and opposing counsel that he intended to appear to contest the tentative ruling sustaining the demurrer without leave to amend. While the Court does not condone these multiple deficiencies, it exercises its discretion to relieve Plaintiff of the consequences of its counsel's failings in the interest of hearing the matter on its merits, particularly because Plaintiff's counsel acted diligently to set aside the order and Defendants have not shown that they will be prejudiced.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano, CSR#8600, e-mail: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Derek O. Meyers, esq. (650-573-9500) of Chauvel & Glatt for Defendant.
No other appearances.

Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs ROBERT SCHMIDT, and ROBERT'S TAX SERVICE, LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Sustain without leave to amend. No opposition filed. Defendants' request for sanctions is denied.

Order is signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
